description: A payment endpoint without idempotency that double-charges a customer when a client retries a timed-out request.
body: Failure mode: a charge request times out at the client, the client retries, and because the server has no idempotency key it executes a second charge, billing the customer twice and creating two ledger entries. Detection signals: duplicate charges with near-identical timestamps; two ledger entries for one order; customer disputes from retries. Do not assume the network delivers each request exactly once. Safer fallback: require an idempotency key, persist it with the result before responding, and return the original result on repeat. Risk boundary: advisory only; resolve a suspected double charge by escalating to a human for refund review, never by automatically issuing or reversing money without the approval gate.
